summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #279 — the ScrollArea agnostic core component for the ars-ui Rust UI library using a state-machine architecture, following established patterns from tooltip, rating_group, and collapsible components.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented and merged the ScrollArea core component with state machine (Idle/Hovering/ScrollActive/ThumbDragging), spec conformance tests, proptest harnesses, and 97.48% line coverage; all 10 CI gates passed and PR was pushed closing #279.

repo: The agnostic core layer must never reference platform APIs (e.g., set_timeout); async/timer behavior must be expressed as named Effect intents (e.g., Effect::AutoHide) and delegated to adapters — matching the tooltip/rating_group pattern.
context_changed is set whenever an apply closure runs, not on value-diff — tests must not assume it reflects a semantic state change.
The @stable toolchain is pinned to 1.96.0 across ars-components; a single pre-existing clippy error in any file blocks all clippy runs for the entire crate.
Delay props that always have a default (like hide delay) should be plain Duration, not Option<Duration>, matching the tooltip/hover_card/avatar convention.
Local scroll_area uses Axis{X,Y} which differs from ars-dom's Axis{Horizontal,Vertical} — a cross-cutting naming inconsistency that is known and deferred.
Insta --accept is unsafe in this repo due to pre-existing i18n-gated highlight snapshots; scroll_area snapshots must be promoted with targeted per-test commands.
Prop parity between the agnostic core and both adapter specs (Leptos and Dioxus) must be explicitly maintained — newly discovered props require updates to all three spec files.

code.finding: The else 0.0 branch in compute_thumb_metrics is unreachable due to an early return above it — justified coverage miss, no logic error.
AutoHide setup closure body is adapter-invoked and cannot be exercised from pure unit tests — justified coverage miss.
MouseEnter outside Hover mode was an uncovered branch caught during audit; covered by adding a dedicated test.
TrackClick for Axis::X and the cross-size branch were uncovered; covered by new targeted tests.
Pre-existing clippy error (map_or_else should be unwrap_or_else) was blocking all clippy runs on the crate; fixed as a side-effect of this PR.

workflow: context_only closures in the state machine framework require explicit `&mut Context` type annotations to compile.
Messages structs must derive PartialEq for state machine equality checks to compile.
Float equality in state machine tests requires an approx comparison helper rather than direct assert_eq!.
Background insta snapshot jobs can race; always clean up background processes before running targeted snapshot promotion commands.
A three-phase post-implementation audit (spec/impl drift, adjacent surfaces, coverage) is the established quality gate pattern for new components.
cargo xci-fast runs 10 gates and is the canonical pre-push validation command for this repo.

friction: The original spec contained a platform set_timeout call inside the agnostic core — a design drift that required identifying and converting to the Effect intent pattern before implementation could proceed correctly.
Insta --accept is blocked by pre-existing i18n-gated snapshots, forcing manual targeted snapshot promotion per test rather than bulk acceptance.
A pre-existing clippy error in pagination/mod.rs silently blocked all clippy runs on ars-components, making it appear as though new code had lint failures.
Background snapshot promotion jobs raced with each other, requiring cleanup before results were reliable.

The scrollbar_cross_size field was computed by axis_metrics but never populated from props or events — only caught during post-implementation audit, requiring a retroactive Props builder and spec updates.

hide_delay was initially typed as u32 milliseconds instead of Duration — caught in user review after implementation was otherwise complete, requiring a full pass across impl, spec, and both adapter specs.
open_items: Adapter implementations (Leptos and Dioxus ScrollArea components) are out of scope for this PR and must be built separately.
Local Axis{X,Y} vs ars-dom Axis{Horizontal,Vertical} naming inconsistency is a known cross-cutting refactor explicitly deferred to a future PR.
The pagination/mod.rs clippy fix was applied as a side-effect; team may prefer it isolated in its own PR for clean history.
End-to-end tests are not applicable for the agnostic core and must be added when the adapter components land.
